skill.name: infrastructure-management
skill.description: Manage network hosts and devices across all UniFi sites. Monitor host status, device configuration, and infrastructure health for comprehensive inventory management.
skill.summary: Review UniFi hosts, devices, site assignments, and infrastructure status.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography indicators, but review found these are false positives in Markdown documentation. The file defines UniFi inventory workflows and tool names only; no executable code, network calls, secret access, prompt injection, or cryptographic usage was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Tool Names
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated backticked tool names and workflow references as shell execution. These lines document available UniFi tools and usage steps, and they do not contain executable code or command interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported locations are plain Markdown bullets and numbered steps. There is no script body, shell metacharacter use, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Indicator
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged the frontmatter description as weak cryptography. The line only describes infrastructure inventory management and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is descriptive metadata, not executable code. No cryptographic primitive or API usage appears in the reviewed skill file.

content.value_statement: Network teams need a clear view of hosts, devices, sites, and status across UniFi environments. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through inventory, status review, and site-based infrastructure analysis.
actual_capabilities: List hosts across all UniFi sites.
Retrieve detailed host information and status.
List network devices across all UniFi sites.
Retrieve device configuration and status details.
Filter hosts and devices by site.
Support inventory reports, troubleshooting checks, and capacity reviews.
limitations: Requires connected UniFi tools and valid access permissions.
Documents inspection workflows, but does not provide implementation code.
Does not state that it can change device configuration.
Does not replace dedicated monitoring, alerting, or security controls.

use_cases.title: Daily Infrastructure Review
use_cases.description: Check host and device status across all UniFi sites before operational work begins.

use_cases.title: Site Inventory Audit
use_cases.description: Compare hosts and devices by site to verify assignments and identify missing documentation.

use_cases.title: Capacity Planning Snapshot
use_cases.description: Summarize resource distribution across sites to support expansion planning.

prompt_templates.title: List All Infrastructure
prompt_templates.prompt: List all hosts and network devices across every UniFi site. Include status and site assignment when available.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Site
prompt_templates.prompt: Show all hosts and devices for the selected site. Summarize status, device roles, and any missing details.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Device Health
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the target device, retrieve its details, and explain any configuration or status concerns in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Infrastructure Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an infrastructure audit summary by site. Include host counts, device counts, status patterns, configuration concerns, and follow-up actions.

output_examples.input: Show me all network devices and their status.
output_examples.output: A site-grouped device summary with each device name, role, current status, and any visible configuration concerns.

output_examples.input: How many hosts and devices do I have per site?
output_examples.output: A concise per-site count of hosts and devices, followed by notes about uneven distribution or missing assignments.

output_examples.input: Get detailed information about a specific device.
output_examples.output: A device detail report covering status, site, configuration fields, and recommended checks for inconsistencies.

best_practices: Start with broad inventory lists before requesting detailed device or host records.
Use site filters when reviewing assignments, capacity, or local incidents.
Validate important findings against current UniFi data before making operational changes.
anti_patterns: Do not assume this skill can modify devices unless a separate tool confirms write access.
Do not use stale inventory results for incident decisions without refreshing status.
Do not expose sensitive network details in shared reports without review.

faq.question: What UniFi information can this skill help review?
faq.answer: It can help review hosts, network devices, site assignments, status, configuration details, and infrastructure inventory summaries.

faq.question: Can it filter results by site?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflows include host and device lookup by specific UniFi site.

faq.question: Can it change device settings?
faq.answer: No change workflow is documented. Treat this skill as read-only unless another authorized tool provides write operations.

faq.question: Does it include monitoring alerts?
faq.answer: No. It supports status review and health checks, but it is not a full alerting system.

faq.question: Is this useful for troubleshooting?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide device lookup, configuration review, host connectivity checks, and diagnostic summaries.

faq.question: Which assistants can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
